nodes.title: Epic: Vertical approval flow
nodes.description:
Deliver an approval flow as demonstrable slices.

## Outcome Trace
- PLAN-SLICE-DELIVER: administrator | approval command | approve a draft | durable approved record | query by ID | denied actor remains unchanged | integration and security evidence | Task 1, final Task 2.

## Success Criteria
- PLAN-SLICE-DELIVER passes at the real command seam and final gate.

nodes.title: Task 1: Approve and retrieve one draft
nodes.description:
Deliver the first working approval slice.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/product/approval-product-contract.md` revision 3.
- Spec: `docs/specs/approval-design.md`.
- Outcome IDs: PLAN-SLICE-DELIVER.
- External ref: None.
- Why this slice exists: prove authority and persistence together.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: administrator invokes `approve ID`.
- Observable result: the command reports approval.
- Durable result / find-again path: `show ID` returns the approved record.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: a member is denied and the draft remains unchanged.

## Domain Contract
- Only server-resolved administrators approve; approval is idempotent.

## Files
- Create: `src/approval.py`.
- Test: `tests/test_approval.py`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: `src/approval.py`, `tests/test_approval.py`.
- Exclusive resources: approval command contract.
- Capacity resources: None.

## Interfaces
- Produces: APPROVAL-API.
- Consumes: None.
- Hard ordering constraints: None.
- `approve(id, actor) -> ApprovalResult`; `show(id) -> Draft`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given an administrator and draft, `approve ID` persists approval and `show ID` returns it.
- Given a member, the command is denied without mutation.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Run `pytest tests/test_approval.py`; this exercises command, authority, persistence, and retrieval in one task.

## Implementation Notes
1. Write the denied and approved integration tests.
2. Implement the minimal command and repository behavior.
3. Run the named test and commit the verified slice.

nodes.title: Task 2: Final approval outcome gate
nodes.description:
Independently verify the product outcome.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/product/approval-product-contract.md` revision 3.
- Spec: `docs/specs/approval-design.md`.
- Outcome IDs: PLAN-SLICE-DELIVER.
- External ref: None.
- Why this slice exists: prevent lower-level evidence substitution.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: reviewer invokes the shipped command.
- Observable result: approved and denied journeys match the contract.
- Durable result / find-again path: the reviewer retrieves the approved record after restart.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: any failed or untested result leaves the outcome open.

## Domain Contract
- Only current-commit integration and security evidence can pass the gate.

## Files
- Create: `tests/test_approval_outcome.py`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: `tests/test_approval_outcome.py`.
- Exclusive resources: final approval evidence.
- Capacity resources: None.

## Interfaces
- Produces: None.
- Consumes: APPROVAL-API.
- Hard ordering constraints: None.
- Reads the public `approve` and `show` commands.

## Acceptance Criteria
- PLAN-SLICE-DELIVER is PASS only when approved, denied, persistence, and restart evidence pass.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Run `pytest tests/test_approval_outcome.py` against the integrated command.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add current-commit outcome tests.
2. Run them and record the evidence artifact.
